summary: Matched the 68-byte function `lbSnap_8001D350` in `src/melee/lb/lbsnap.c`, raising it to 100% in the PR report. The implementation clarified part of `lb_80433380` by changing the struct layout around offset `0x48` from an integer array placeholder to a pointer to per-entry records, then used that nested record to update and return a state field. The scratches config was refreshed so many historical scratch entries around this region moved from percentage scores to `OK`, including the `0x8001D350` candidates.
what_changed: Added `struct Unk80433380_48` with `s32 unk0` at offset `0` and padding through `0x408`, indicating a per-entry stride of `0x408` bytes.

Adjusted `struct Unk80433380`: replaced `char filler0[0x40]; int unk40[3];` with `char filler0[0x48]; struct Unk80433380_48* x48;`, preserving later fields `x4C_cardState[2]` and `x54_stateChanged[3]`.
Implemented `int lbSnap_8001D350(int i)`: takes `&lb_80433380.x48[i]`, checks `ptr->unk0 == 0 && lb_80433380.x54_stateChanged[i]`, sets `ptr->unk0 = 8` when true, and returns `ptr->unk0`.

Updated `config/GALE01/scratches.txt` to mark numerous scratch candidates as `OK`, including all listed `func_8001D350`/`Untitled` candidates at `0x8001D350` that were previously partial or 100% percentage entries.
smart_moves: Resolved the target function by improving the inferred global struct layout rather than forcing local code shape alone; recognizing offset `0x48` as a pointer enabled clean indexed access in `lbSnap_8001D350`.
Introduced a nested placeholder struct with only the known field named, while preserving unknown bytes as padding. This keeps the match evidence-grounded and leaves room for future field discovery.

Used the same index `i` for both the per-record array at `x48[i]` and the existing `x54_stateChanged[i]` array, exposing the relationship between snapshot/card state-change flags and per-entry status values.
Kept unknown names generic (`Unk80433380_48`, `unk0`, `x48`) instead of over-naming behavior that the small function does not fully prove.
decomp_lessons: For small functions, a correct global struct interpretation can be the main matching unlock; check whether suspicious arrays or padding in an existing global should actually be pointers or nested records.
When a function indexes a pointer field and the generated code depends on stride, define a minimal nested struct with explicit size/padding to force the right addressing pattern.
A state-setting idiom of `if field == 0 && flag[index] then field = constant; return field;` can often be represented directly once the surrounding struct offsets are accurate.

Scratch database updates may accompany a successful source match and can convert older partial or percentage scratch attempts to `OK`; do not treat these as separate source-code matches unless the diff shows source changes.
naming_conventions: Unknown global-derived struct names followed the project placeholder style `Unk<address>_<offset>`, here `Unk80433380_48` for data hanging off `lb_80433380` at offset `0x48`.
Struct fields used offset-based names such as `x48`, `x4C_cardState`, and `x54_stateChanged` where semantics are incomplete or partially known.
Unknown scalar field retained the conservative name `unk0`; no semantic name was introduced without stronger evidence.
Matched function retained the address-bearing decomp name `lbSnap_8001D350`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed the parent struct padding from `0x40` plus an `int[3]` placeholder to `0x48` plus a pointer field, aligning `x48`, `x4C_cardState`, and `x54_stateChanged` with the apparent data layout needed by the target function.
Used a nested struct padded to `0x408` bytes so array indexing `lb_80433380.x48[i]` matches the original record stride.
Represented the conditional update directly with short-circuit `&&`, likely matching the original branch structure for checking zero state before consulting `x54_stateChanged[i]`.
Returned the same field after possible mutation, avoiding extra temporaries beyond the record pointer.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further define `Unk80433380_48`, `x48`, or `unk0` in `lbsnap.c`.
Search for other uses of `lb_80433380.x48` to confirm the `0x408` record layout and discover additional fields.
Check whether `lbSnap_8001D350` eventually receives a semantic name once snapshot/card-state behavior is better understood.
Review adjacent functions `lbSnap_8001D2BC` and nearby `lbGx_8001E2F8` work for related struct-layout refinements.
